subject: Physics
chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

concepts.title: Wave-Particle Duality
concepts.description: The dual nature of radiation and matter proposes that every particle or quantum entity can be described as both a particle and a wave, depending on how it is observed and the experiment conducted.

concepts.title: Photoelectric Effect
concepts.description: The photoelectric effect is a phenomenon where light hitting a metal surface can cause the emission of electrons from the surface, and this effect is a direct consequence of the particle nature of light.

concepts.title: de Broglie Wavelength
concepts.description: The de Broglie wavelength of a particle is given by the formula λ = h / p, where λ is the wavelength, h is Planck's constant, and p is the momentum of the particle, illustrating the wave nature of matter.

concepts.title: Einstein's Photoelectric Equation
concepts.description: To remember Einstein's photoelectric equation, K_max = hf - φ, use the mnemonic 'King Henry Finds Gold': K_max (kinetic energy) = h (Planck's constant) * f (frequency) - φ (work function).

concepts.title: Confusing Intensity with Energy
concepts.description: Students often confuse intensity with energy in the context of the photoelectric effect. Intensity is related to the number of photons, while energy is related to the frequency of the photons. Make sure to distinguish between these two concepts.

concepts.title: Planck's Constant and Electron Charge
concepts.description: Remember the units of Planck's constant (h = 6.626 * 10^-34 J s) and the charge of an electron (e = 1.602 * 10^-19 C), as these values are frequently used in calculations related to the dual nature of radiation and matter.

concepts.title: Compton Scattering
concepts.description: Compton scattering is an experiment that demonstrates the particle nature of light, where X-ray photons collide with electrons, resulting in a transfer of energy and momentum, and a change in the wavelength of the scattered photon.

concepts.title: Davisson-Germer Experiment
concepts.description: The Davisson-Germer experiment is a historical study that confirmed the wave nature of electrons, where electrons passing through a crystal lattice produced an interference pattern, similar to X-ray diffraction, demonstrating the wave-like behavior of particles.
